name: Paella#2
recipeIngredient: 2 small Fillet Turbot, Sole, Red Snapper or Trout, cut into 1-inch chunks
1/2 Chorizo sausage, cut into small chunks
14-ounce can Artichoke hearts, drained, rinsed, and quartered
1 medium Red pepper, cut into 1/2-inch strips
2 oz Madeira wine or sweet sherry
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large, deep, flat pan, combine the rice and water or stock. Cover and bake in an oven set to 350°F (175°C) to 400°F (200°C) until almost all the water is absorbed.
2. In a sauté pan, melt the butter over medium heat. Add the diced carrot, celery, and onion. Cook until the vegetables are soft and the onions are translucent. Remove from the heat and set aside.
3. Remove the rice pan from the oven. Mix in the sautéed vegetables, shrimp, clams, fish fillets, chicken, sausages, artichoke hearts, red pepper strips, and garlic. Stir in the lemon juice, saffron, and Madeira wine or sweet sherry.
4. Arrange the mussels around the edge of the pan. Sprinkle diced tomato and halved black olives over the top.
5. Cover and return to the oven until the mussels open and the rice, fish, and chicken are cooked through. The rice should be dry, not mushy.
6. Season with salt and pepper to taste. Serve in large plates, arranging the mussels around the edge of each plate. Garnish with parsley and lemon wedges.
